title: quercus sessilis var. undulata
intro: Quercus sessilis var. undulata, also known as the Japanese oak, is a variety of evergreen shrub or small tree that belongs to the Quercus genus. Traditionally, the leaves of Quercus sessilis var. undulata have been used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are believed to provide various health benefits. The leaves are said to have medicinal properties, including being a natural astringent and having antiseptic and antimicrobial effects, making them useful for treating skin conditions and promoting wound healing. Quercus sessilis var. undulata leaves are often prepared as a tea or infusion, and can also be dried and used in herbal supplements or applied topically as a poultice.
